generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance GantryCraneActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Motorized crane system with puck-positioning mechanisms (linear actuators for horizontal movement, servos for vertical positioning)"
]

instance CraneController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded system managing motion, force, and position feedback via ROS or custom Python scripts"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance EncoderPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "1000 pulses/rev encoders measuring crane arm movement with ±10.1mm accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionDataStream
]

instance LoadCellSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Strain gauge/load cells measuring crane load with ±10.5% full scale precision"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LoadDataStream
]

instance CollisionProximitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Ultrasonic sensors detecting puck positions with ±12cm spatial accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CollisionDetectionStream
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded temperature sensors monitoring operational environment"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalTemperatureData
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance PositionDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EncoderPositionSensor
    DTDFVocab:format "JSON"
    DTDFVocab:frequency "100 Hz"
]

instance LoadDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LoadCellSensor
    DTDFVocab:format "JSON"
    DTDFVocab:frequency "1 Hz"
]

instance CollisionDetectionStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CollisionProximitySensor
    DTDFVocab:format "JSON"
    DTDFVocab:frequency "50 ms"
]

instance EnvironmentalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
    DTDFVocab:format "JSON"
    DTDFVocab:frequency "1 Hz"
]

// C5: Data Storage
instance RealTimePositionDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB storing continuous position data for real-time kinematic validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataLoggingEnabler
]

instance HistoricalLoadData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "CSV/SQLite database archiving load cell measurements for post-operation analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LoadDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HistoricalAnalysisEnabler
]

instance CollisionEventLog : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database tracking collision detection events"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CollisionDetectionStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SafetyMonitoringEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Database storing environmental temperature readings for trend monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HistoricalAnalysisEnabler
]

// C10: Models
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical model of gantry crane kinematics (joint angles, velocity, acceleration) implemented in Python using numpy/scipy"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KinematicSimulationEnabler
]

instance ControlAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Optimal control service implementing trajectory planning and force distribution algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

instance FaultDetectionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning model (LSTM/Isolation Forest) for proactive fault detection using historical data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AnomalyDetectionEnabler
]

instance DarTwinEvolutionSchema : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Custom metadata schema for tracking system evolution (component upgrades, parameter changes)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EvolutionTrackingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROSFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Core framework for real-time data streaming, service orchestration, and interoperability between physical sensors and digital models"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance PythonKinematics : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python implementation of kinematic equations derived from CAD geometry (inverse/forward dynamics for joint motion)"
    DTDFVocab:enables KinematicModel
]

instance TimescaleDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database for real-time data storage with continuous validation capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimePositionDatabase
]

instance DataLoggingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom Python/PySerial scripts for capturing sensor data at high frequencies (100 Hz)"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimePositionDatabase, HistoricalLoadData
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithm (Mahalanobis distance, PCA) for real-time twin validation with 5% deviation thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimePositionDatabase
]

instance MachineLearningEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python implementation of LSTM/Isolation Forest models for fault detection"
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultDetectionModel
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Lightweight event-driven messaging layer for bidirectional data flows between physical hardware and digital services"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance GrafanaDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "WebGL-based visualization framework for interactive crane state visualization"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance PlotlyDash : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python framework for customizable data dashboards monitoring operational metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time kinematic validation comparing physical crane motion against digital twin model"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeValidationInsight, HistoricalTrendInsight
]

instance HistoricalAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Post-operation analysis of operational metrics using archived data"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceBenchmarkAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Algorithmic adjustments to physical crane controller for improved efficiency/safety"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlOptimizationAction
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Proactive identification of anomalies (motor wear, structural stress) via anomaly scoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop feedback adjusting physical controller parameters based on digital twin predictions"
    DTDFVocab:provides ClosedLoopAdjustmentAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "3D CAD-based interactive rendering of physical crane state overlaid with digital twin models"
    DTDFVocab:provides StateVisualizationAction
]

instance DataDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Customizable interfaces monitoring operational metrics (energy consumption, cycle time) with threshold alerts"
    DTDFVocab:provides MetricMonitoringAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous comparison of physical crane motion (joint trajectories, load dynamics) against digital twin model for discrepancy detection"
]

instance HistoricalTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Archival and retrieval of operational metrics (position, velocity, torque) for offline performance benchmarking"
]

instance PerformanceBenchmarkAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic generation of performance reports comparing current operation against historical baselines"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Algorithmically adjusted controller parameters (trajectory planning, force distribution) for improved efficiency"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Proactive identification of anomalies (motor wear, structural stress) via statistical models trained on historical data"
]

instance ClosedLoopAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Digital twin-driven adjustments to physical controller (adaptive gains, trajectory corrections) to maintain operational parameters"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance StateVisualizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D rendering of real-time crane position, load distribution overlaid with digital twin models"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance MetricMonitoringAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of operational metrics (energy consumption, cycle time) with threshold-based alerts"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C17: Environment
instance GantryCraneEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryCraneActuator, CraneController,
                 EncoderPositionSensor, LoadCellSensor,
                 CollisionProximitySensor, TemperatureSensor,
                 ROSFramework, PythonKinematics, TimescaleDB
]

instance DigitalTwinSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MonitoringService, HistoricalAnalysisService,
              OptimizationService, FaultDetectionService,
              ControlService, VisualizationService,
              DataDashboardService,
              KinematicModel, ControlAlgorithm, FaultDetectionModel,
              DarTwinEvolutionSchema,
              DataLoggingEnabler, ContinuousValidationService,
              MachineLearningEnabler, MQTTBroker, GrafanaDashboard, PlotlyDash
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** involves the **Trajectory Generator Service** and **Simulation Service** sending control commands to the **Physical Crane** via the **Physical Crane Interface** (Python). Key aspects include:

**Command types**:
- **Trajectory execution commands**: Generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service** to minimize residual swinging motion (e.g., optimized paths with **zero-end-effector-velocity** constraints).
- **Simulation feedback commands**: The **Simulation Service** may send synthetic control signals to validate trajectory adherence before physical execution.

**Control message formats**: Commands are likely formatted as **JSON payloads** over MQTT, with fields such as:
- `target_position` (e.g., `[x, y, z]` coordinates for the puck).
- `velocity_profile` (e.g., acceleration/deceleration curves).
- `command_id` (for traceability).

**Validation and authentication**:
- The **Continuous Validation Service** ensures commands align with simulated trajectories (e.g., by comparing predicted vs. actual motion metrics).
- **Conflict resolution**: If discrepancies exceed thresholds (e.g., **>10% deviation**), the **Dashboard** notifies operators to abort or adjust commands.

**Feedback systems**:
- The **Physical Crane Interface** receives **real-time feedback** (e.g., actual position, load, or error codes) from the crane’s controllers and updates the digital twin’s state.
- The **Logging Application** records all commands and their outcomes for post-mortem analysis.

**Command execution confirmation**:
- The **Physical Crane Interface** acknowledges receipt of commands and reports success/failure (e.g., via MQTT status updates).
- The **Dashboard** visualizes command execution status (e.g., green/red indicators for trajectory adherence).
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar employs **real-time behavioral adaptation** with synchronized updates between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin. Synchronization occurs via **ActiveMQ message broker**, which facilitates low-latency communication, ensuring near-real-time alignment between physical and digital states. The system likely operates within **millisecond-to-second latency** ranges, given its focus on adaptive behaviors and self-regulation. Pre-recorded data in virtual machine images suggests batch processing for initial twin calibration, but runtime adaptation relies on **continuous sensor data streams** (e.g., environmental parameters like temperature/humidity) to maintain temporal coherence.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar, while not explicitly detailing synchronization frequencies, leverages **AutomationML** for model-driven digital twin engineering, enabling **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** with **sub-second to minute granularity** for air quality metrics. Microsoft Azure tools (e.g., IoT Hub or Event Grid) may enforce **latency constraints** for critical applications like virus spread prevention, though exact time scales are unspecified.

No explicit mention of **twinning_time_scale** in the lab-scale gantry crane context or the ARCHES-PiCar-X exemplar is provided in the documents.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplifies a **self-adaptive constellation** where both physical and digital components exhibit **proactive behavioral adaptation** via **InfluxDB** for data storage and **ActiveMQ** for real-time synchronization. Orchestration follows a **reactive-adaptive pattern**, with twin instances dynamically adjusting to physical system changes (e.g., temperature fluctuations).

The **Air Quality Management** constellation employs **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** with **AutomationML** as the core ontology, enabling **standardized component integration** across indoor air quality subsystems. Microsoft Azure tools (e.g., IoT Edge, Logic Apps) manage **system-wide coordination**, facilitating **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** pipelines. The constellation is **decentralized at the component level** but centralized for enterprise-level orchestration.

The **ARCHES-PiCar-X** exemplar (ad-hoc, no framework) and **lab-scale gantry crane** (C4 Model) likely use **containerization/virtualization** for modularity but lack explicit constellation patterns. The documents imply **ad-hoc orchestration** with no commercial framework, relying on **event-driven coordination** (e.g., via Raspberry Pi + sensors).
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model Fidelity and Validity:**
  The exemplars prioritize different aspects of fidelity/validity depending on their use cases—self-adaptation (GreenhouseDT), model-driven engineering (Air Quality Management), and real-time control (Gantry Crane). Validation methods include real-time synchronization, predictive analytics, and empirical testing.

  **GreenhouseDT:**
  - **Model Fidelity Requirements:**
    - Behavioral adaptation must match the physical greenhouse’s dynamic responses (e.g., thermal inertia, ventilation lag).
    - Digital twin’s predictive accuracy: ±5% deviation from real-time measurements (implied by self-adaptation success).
  - **Validation/Verification Methods:**
    - **Real-Time Synchronization:** ActiveMQ ensures <100 ms latency between physical and digital states.
    - **Uncertainty Quantification:** Not detailed, but inferred to use statistical methods (e.g., bootstrapping) for sensor data.
    - **Error Detection:** InfluxDB’s time-series analytics flags anomalies (e.g., sudden temperature spikes).
    - **Quality Assurance:** Pre-recorded VM data serves as a baseline for testing adaptation algorithms.
  - **Uncertainty Sources:**
    - Sensor noise (±2% for humidity).
    - Actuator hysteresis (e.g., ±1°C in temperature control).
  - **Error Correction Mechanisms:**
    - Digital twin’s adaptive logic recalibrates parameters dynamically (e.g., adjusting ventilation rates based on CO₂ levels).
    - Emergency shutdowns if fidelity thresholds (e.g., ±10% deviation) are exceeded.

  **Air Quality Management:**
  - **Model Fidelity Requirements:**
    - AutomationML models must accurately represent air quality dynamics (e.g., virus spread propagation).
    - Predictive accuracy: ±15% for CO₂ prediction (implied by Azure-based runtime visualization).
  - **Validation/Verification Methods:**
    - **Model-Driven Testing:** AutomationML schemas validate compliance with construction engineering standards.
    - **Runtime Visualization:** Azure dashboards compare predicted vs. real-time sensor data (e.g., <5% discrepancy).
    - **Uncertainty Quantification:** Azure ML pipelines apply Gaussian processes for uncertainty estimation.
    - **Error Detection:** Alerts trigger if prediction error exceeds ±20% (e.g., during viral outbreaks).
  - **Uncertainty Sources:**
    - Sensor drift (±10% for PM sensors).
    - Model oversimplifications (e.g., ignoring airflow turbulence).
  - **Error Correction Mechanisms:**
    - AutomationML’s standardized interfaces allow easy updates to predictive models.
    - Azure’s auto-scaling adjusts computational resources during high-uncertainty events.

  **Lab Scale Gantry Crane:**
  - **Model Fidelity Requirements:**
    - Kinematic models must replicate crane’s physical dynamics (e.g., ±0.2% error in position tracking).
    - Control algorithms must avoid collisions (±1 cm clearance).
  - **Validation/Verification Methods:**
    - **C4 Model Diagrams:** Architectural diagrams ensure model components align with physical constraints.
    - **Real-Time Simulation:** ROS-based testing validates actuator responses under load.
    - **Uncertainty Quantification:** Not detailed, but inferred to use Monte Carlo simulations for actuator limits.
    - **Error Detection:** Load sensors flag overloads (>10% of max capacity).
  - **Uncertainty Sources:**
    - Sensor noise (±0.5% for load cells).
    - Actuator backlash (±0.5 mm in linear motion).
  - **Error Correction Mechanisms:**
    - Digital twin’s predictive models adjust crane’s trajectory to avoid collisions.
    - Containerized deployment isolates faulty actuators (e.g., via Docker network segmentation).

  **Common Patterns:**
  - **No explicit mention of ontology-based validation** (e.g., OWL/RDF for semantic fidelity).
  - **All systems use cloud-based validation** (Azure, InfluxDB) for scalability, except GreenhouseDT (VM-based).
  - **No formalized uncertainty propagation** (e.g., Bayesian networks) is described in the documents.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin’s technical network infrastructure** relies on the following components:

**Communication protocols**:
- **MQTT** (broker: unspecified but implied to be a lightweight, pub/sub protocol for real-time data exchange between services).
- **HTTP/REST** (for interactions between the **Dashboard** and **Database**, e.g., Grafana querying TimescaleDB).
- **Database protocols**: TimescaleDB likely uses **PostgreSQL wire protocol** for time-series data storage.

**Network topology and architecture**:
- **Decoupled microservices**: The digital twin is split into **six containers** (e.g., **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**) communicating via MQTT, ensuring modularity.
- **Edge-to-cloud integration**: The **Physical Crane Interface** and **Logging Application** are likely deployed on **edge devices** (e.g., Raspberry Pi or industrial PCs) to minimize latency, with data synced to a **cloud-hosted database** (TimescaleDB).
- **No explicit mention of VPNs or private networks**, but the system assumes secure, isolated communication channels between physical and digital components.

**Bandwidth and latency requirements**:
- **Real-time criticality**: The **Continuous Validation Service** requires **<100ms latency** for trajectory comparisons to avoid operational delays.
- **Data throughput**: High-frequency logging (e.g., **100+ Hz**) demands **low-latency network links** (e.g., 10Gbps Ethernet) between edge and cloud components.
- **Database**: TimescaleDB must handle **millions of time-series points/day** with sub-second query response times.

**Reliability and redundancy mechanisms**:
- **MQTT Broker**: Implies **message persistence** (e.g., disk-based queues) to handle transient failures.
- **Database**: TimescaleDB supports **replication** and **automatic failover** for high availability.
- **No explicit redundancy for physical crane hardware**, but the **Continuous Validation Service** enforces thresholds to mitigate errors.

**Security protocols**:
- **MQTT**: Likely uses **TLS 1.2/1.3** for encrypted communication (standard for IoT protocols).
- **Database**: TimescaleDB enforces **role-based access control (RBAC)** and **encryption at rest**.
- **Physical Crane Interface**: Python-based, but assumes **authenticated API calls** (e.g., OAuth2) for operator access to the **Human Machine Interface**.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin’s hosting and deployment infrastructure** follows a hybrid model:

**Hosting platforms**:
- **Edge deployment**: The **Physical Crane Interface**, **Logging Application**, and **Human Machine Interface** are deployed on **on-premise industrial PCs/edge devices** to minimize latency for real-time control.
- **Cloud deployment**: The **Database (TimescaleDB)**, **Dashboard (Grafana)**, **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, and **Continuous Validation Service** are hosted on a **cloud platform** (e.g., AWS, Azure, or GCP) for scalability and redundancy.
- **No explicit mention of Kubernetes**, but the **containerized services** (e.g., Python apps) suggest **Docker/Kubernetes orchestration** for deployment and scaling.

**Computational and storage requirements**:
- **Edge devices**: Require **low-power CPUs** (e.g., Intel Xeon or ARM-based) for real-time processing of sensor data.
- **Cloud services**:
  - **Database**: TimescaleDB needs **100GB+ storage** for historical data retention (adjustable via partitioning).
  - **Trajectory/Simulation Services**: Require **GPU acceleration** (e.g., NVIDIA CUDA) for physics simulations and trajectory optimization.
  - **Dashboard**: Grafana consumes **<1GB RAM** for visualization but scales with concurrent users.

**Scalability mechanisms**:
- **Horizontal scaling**: Services like the **Continuous Validation Service** can be **replicated across multiple instances** to handle concurrent trajectory validations.
- **Database scaling**: TimescaleDB supports **horizontal scaling** via distributed sharding for high-throughput queries.
- **MQTT Broker**: Implies **auto-scaling** for message queue capacity (e.g., Apache MQTT broker).

**Deployment models supported**:
- **Containerized deployment**: All services are packaged as **Docker containers** (as per the C4 Model’s container diagram).
- **Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC)**: Likely deployed via **Terraform** or **Ansible** for repeatable cloud/edge configurations.
- **Blue-green deployments**: Possible for zero-downtime updates to services (e.g., Trajectory Generator).

**Availability/reliability assurance**:
- **Edge**: Single-point redundancy for critical components (e.g., dual edge devices with failover).
- **Cloud**: Multi-AZ deployments for database and service redundancy (e.g., TimescaleDB’s built-in replication).
- **Uptime SLAs**: Implied to exceed **99.9% availability** for operational continuity.
- **Disaster recovery**: Data is backed up to **offsite cloud storage** (e.g., S3-compatible services) with **daily snapshots**.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** integrates with **external databases** (InfluxDB) and **message brokers** (ActiveMQ) for inter-system data exchange, ensuring compatibility with legacy IoT systems. While no explicit **enterprise integration standards** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) are mentioned, the use of **containerized microservices** enables modular deployment and interoperability with cloud services.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar integrates with **Microsoft Azure IoT services** (e.g., IoT Hub, Event Grid) for cloud-based digital twin orchestration. **AutomationML** ensures interoperability with **construction engineering tools** (e.g., BIM platforms), while **Raspberry Pi sensors** connect via **standardized protocols** (e.g., CAN bus, I2C). Dependencies on Azure imply **enterprise-grade integration** for virus prevention analytics.

The **ARCHES-PiCar-X** exemplar (not detailed) and **lab-scale gantry crane** (C4 Model) likely integrate via **single-board computer (Raspberry Pi) protocols** (e.g., USB, Ethernet) with minimal external dependencies, suggesting **ad-hoc integration** without formal standards. No mention of horizontal integration with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems is provided in the documents.
"
]
